reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own answer to 84 - 37 is certainly correct, and in all previous games the others’ simple arithmetic has also been correct with this same capsule pattern. If I pass (or verify), it’s very likely no one volunteers in Round 1 and there’s a 25% chance I die from random elimination, while verifying would also cost a coin without reducing that risk. The probability that any of the others’ answers is wrong is much smaller than 25%, so volunteering now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my survival odds while keeping all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q3  
REASONING: All four questions currently have only single-solver capsules, so one mistake would kill whoever volunteers. I’m very confident in my own answer to Q2 (12 - 7), so I’d rather spend 1 coin to independently check someone else’s work. Verifying Q3 again should give us multi-endorsed capsules on Q1 and Q3 (assuming C keeps checking Q1 as in previous games), making it safer for someone else to volunteer while I preserve my survival at low cost.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: All four questions (Q1–Q4) currently have only single-endorsed capsules, so it’s too risky to volunteer now. Verifying costs just 1 coin and doesn’t change the random-death risk if no one volunteers. I already know my own Q3 answer (16 + 67) is correct, so the best way to build early consensus on the full password is to cross-check Q1-A before anyone takes the lethal risk of volunteering.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
